title: Last Updated column doesn't update when replies are added in Conversations
excerpt:
Knowing when someone replies in conversations are essential to the workflow and the reason why there is a Last Updated column in place as a quick view. 
Problem is: THE LAST UPDATED COLUMN DOESN’T UPDATE WHEN SOMEONE REP…

posts.author: hGX5a4 (hGX5a4)
posts.cookedHtml:
Knowing when someone replies in conversations are essential to the workflow and the reason why there is a Last Updated column in place as a quick view.

Problem is: THE LAST UPDATED COLUMN DOESN’T UPDATE WHEN SOMEONE REPLIES.

Pls advise and when can we expect a fix to this bug?!

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @hGX5a4 - this is annoying for sure. What we have done to circumvent this is to have a hidden date column called (last update), and use the following custom automation:

image1245×126 9.7 KB

In turn, this will update the last Updated column.

Hope this helps
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: hGX5a4 (hGX5a4)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ok, but wouldn’t this count towards our automations number for the month?

When can we expect this bug to be fixed? This is pretty ridiculous.

posts.author: arnoldprasad (Arnold P)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have the same question and same issue.

Here is Monday’s response to an obvious bug. The activity log doesn’t even get updated.

image455×692 43.8 KB

Why have a last updated column that doesn’t work. It’s very important to our sales team.

posts.author: mtatevosian (Michael Tatevosian)
posts.cookedHtml: It seems like the column “Last Updated” now does reflect a date when someone last replied back on an item. I just tested it and it seems to work OK.
